title: andira coriacea
intro: Andira coriacea, also known as the Brazilian rosewood or Andiroba, is a tree species native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its oil-rich seeds. The health benefits of Andira coriacea include reducing skin irritations, soothing joint pain, and aiding in the treatment of wounds, as well as exhibiting potential antimalarial properties. Andira coriacea is commonly prepared into herbal preparations, such as essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
